title: Cephaelis dichroa
intro: Cephaelis dichroa, also known as dichroa, is a plant species native to China and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties, making it beneficial for treating fever, sore throats, and infections. Cephaelis dichroa contains bioactive constituents such as dichroanone, dichroanone acetate, and other alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional Chinese medicine formulas to treat various health conditions.
